enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a video request from Brian Delacruz about social anxiety disorder. He attributes social anxiety to insecurity and people-pleasing rooted in upbringing, and advises going to public places not to perform but to observe others from a detached, confident 'king' mindset. He also ties the advice back to a prior request Brian made about a love triangle, suggesting Brian needs to stop seeking external validation entirely.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: rooted in insecurity from guilt-inducing parenting, not a clinical disorder per se
go to public places to watch people, not to impress them — divert attention outward
adopt a 'king' mindset — you're there for yourself, not to perform for others
practice holding eye contact with strangers to build a sense of owning the room
switching up your look can reset how people perceive and approach you
stop trying to validate yourself through others' reactions; just be present
steer clear — she's manipulative; find someone who accepts you as you are

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I probably still have social anxiety disorder since every time I go to a public place, I always feel dizzy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Even if I try to eat healthy, those issues are still there.

claims.subject: Brian Delacruz
claims.claim: Brian Delacruz is a kid in college who likes a girl, but she knows another guy likes her too.

claims.subject: Brian Delacruz
claims.claim: The girl told Brian that the one who shows her the most affection will be the one who gets her.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm surprised Brian would ask me about social anxiety disorder if he knows a girl well enough to be in a love triangle.

claims.subject: Brian Delacruz
claims.claim: Brian gets nervous whenever he goes out and feels dizzy when he meets strangers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian's dizziness is related to his anxiety and nervousness around people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian likely has some kind of insecurity, wanting people to feel like he's doing right by them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian's parents probably made him feel guilty, which knocked his confidence down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian feels like he has something to prove because people expect less of him than he thinks he's worth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done much reading on social anxiety disorder, but I saw a movie about it 15 years ago.

claims.subject: Sean Conner
claims.claim: Sean Conner is a famous writer who hadn't left his apartment for 10 years due to social anxiety.

claims.subject: Sean Conner
claims.claim: Sean Conner was tutored by a kid who eventually convinced him to go out to a baseball game, where he felt nervous.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are different kinds of anxiety, like feeling claustrophobic around too many people.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Being around people you can't easily leave can make you feel nervous and dizzy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian's issue is more about insecurity and worrying what people think of him, not just being unused to crowds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: At college age, people often define themselves based on how others perceive them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should pay more attention to others and less to himself, pretending he's invisible.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should practice making eye contact with people and holding their gaze when they notice him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Looking at people and having them look away can make you feel like you own the room.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If someone gets angry at Brian for looking at them, he should still hold his ground.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should act like a king, being the center of the world but not feeling the need to perform for others.

claims.subject: Captain Cook
claims.claim: Captain Cook encountered a chief who sat silently on his ship, ignoring him, acting like a king.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should adopt the attitude of a king, going to places to entertain himself, not others.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should dress simply and stand in the corner at parties, letting people approach him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian might be a people pleaser, trying to make others happy so they like him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian needs to realize he doesn't have to validate his niceness by performing for others.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Some people are assholes but still cool, which might piss Brian off.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should change his appearance to alter how people perceive him.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Having a radical look can make people treat you differently, sometimes with more respect.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian feels like he needs to change his personality, but he should focus on feeling happy instead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should go to events just to watch people, not to interact with them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Watching people interact can be fun, especially if you're high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Girls like guys who are smog and don't try to please everyone.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Brian should avoid the girl who's manipulating him and find someone who likes him for who he is.
